title: Title formats per surface (issue, milestone, PR)
description: Formal shape of titles.yaml — one entry per title surface (per-issue-type issue titles, milestone titles, PR titles). Each entry carries a regex `pattern` consumers compile, a prose `description`, examples, and per-rule validations tagged with severities. Issue and milestone titles are read for board browsing; PR titles follow Conventional Commits for `git log` parsing. Consumed by the create-issue and validate-body skills at filing and edit time. Companion per COR-018.

formats.description: Title-format entries. Keys are surface ids (e.g., `issue-epic`, `milestone`, `pr`); values match $defs.entry.

surface_kind.description: Which surface this title shape applies to. Determines which validations the skill runs (issue → board-browsing checks; pr → Conventional Commits checks).

applies_to_issue_type.description: When `surface_kind: issue`, the issue type whose title this entry governs. Omitted for `milestone` and `pr` surfaces (which apply to a single global surface).

pattern.description: Regex (PCRE-compatible) the title must match. `null` for free-form surfaces (e.g., milestone). Consumers compile against the language they run in; if the regex must differ across languages, encode the divergence here explicitly.

description.description: One-paragraph prose describing the format — what the parts mean, what readers should expect.

examples_good.description: Titles that match the pattern. Illustrative.

examples_bad.description: Titles that should be rejected and why. Helps consumers verify their regex catches the listed cases.

validations.description: Per-rule validations the skill runs against a title on this surface. Each rule is prose paired with a severity token; severity dispatch happens via the validation-severity schema.
